text: This research explores the implementation of religious character education programs and assesses their tangible influence on students' daily lives. Employing a descriptive qualitative methodology, data were gathered through semi-structured interviews, field observations, and comprehensive documentation. The findings indicate that while the religious character education program at MI Nurul Yaqin Surabaya is well-structured and functioning smoothly, there remains significant potential for refinement to enhance its overall effectiveness. To build on this progress, it is recommended that the madrasa conduct rigorous periodic evaluations, provide specialized teacher training to improve pedagogical delivery, and foster stronger partnerships with parents to reinforce religious values at home. By adopting these measures, MI Nurul Yaqin can ensure its character education initiatives meet their strategic objectives and foster holistic student development.
